meta.name: Indian Chicken Curry
meta.subtitle: Onion-tomato masala base with warm spices; weeknight-friendly

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/chicken_curry.json
notes: Designed for deterministic adaptation: tadka/bloom-spices, onion-tomato masala, optional yogurt finish.

summary: A foundational Indian chicken curry built on an onion-tomato masala with cumin, coriander, turmeric, and garam masala. Finish with a little lemon (and optional yogurt) for balance.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a main with rice/flatbread; 5–6 with sides.

optional.label: Pressure cooker / Instant Pot
optional.notes: Optional: speeds up cooking; not required for this baseline.

signals.freezerFriendly: yes (freeze without yogurt finish for best texture)

notes: Dairy is optional if finishing with yogurt or cream.
Use dairy-free yogurt (coconut/soy) for a dairy-free version.

ingredients.label: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs
notes: Chicken breast works too; reduce simmer time to avoid drying.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste; you will season in layers.

ingredients.label: Ghee
ingredients.notes: Optional but classic; replace with oil to keep dairy-free.

ingredients.label: Kashmiri chili powder (or mild chili powder)
ingredients.notes: Adjust for heat; Kashmiri is more color than burn.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock (or water)
ingredients.notes: Add more for a thinner curry.

ingredients.label: Garam masala
ingredients.notes: Add near the end for best aroma.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt
notes: Optional for a tangy, creamy finish.
Temper first and add off-heat to prevent curdling.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Or substitute lime, amchur, or a little tamarind.

steps.title: Prep and season
instructions: Pat chicken dry and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Season chicken with 1 tsp of the salt (reserve the rest for adjusting later).
Dice onion; mince garlic; grate ginger.

steps.title: Build the onion base
instructions: Heat a heavy pot over medium heat.
Add oil (and ghee if using).
Add onions and a pinch of salt; cook, stirring often, until deeply golden (10–15 minutes).
If onions start to stick, splash in 1–2 tbsp water and scrape up browned bits.
checks: Onions should be golden-brown, not pale.
Browning here drives the curry’s depth.

steps.title: Bloom aromatics and spices
instructions: Add garlic and ginger; cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
Add cumin, coriander, turmeric, Kashmiri chili, and black pepper.
Stir continuously for 20–30 seconds to bloom spices without burning.
checks: Spices should smell fragrant and toasted, not bitter or burnt.
Lower heat immediately if spices darken too fast.

steps.title: Cook the tomato masala
instructions: Add crushed tomatoes and stir well.
Simmer uncovered 8–12 minutes until the masala thickens and you see oil/fat separating at the edges.
This step removes raw tomato flavor and concentrates spice.
checks: Masala should look thicker and darker, not watery.
Oil separation at edges is a good sign of doneness.

steps.title: Simmer chicken until tender
instructions: Add chicken and stir to coat in the masala.
Pour in stock/water, bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer 18–25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and tender.

temperature.notes: Chicken is safe at 165°F; thighs remain tender a bit higher.

checks: Sauce should be gently bubbling, not rapidly boiling.
Chicken should be opaque and tender; adjust liquid for desired thickness.

steps.title: Finish, balance, and serve
instructions: Stir in garam masala and simmer 1 minute.
Turn off heat. Add lemon juice.
Optional yogurt finish (anti-curdle): temper yogurt with a few spoonfuls of hot curry liquid, then stir in off-heat.
Taste and adjust salt, heat, and acidity. Garnish with cilantro if using.
checks: Add yogurt off-heat to prevent curdling.
If curry tastes flat, add a touch more lemon or salt.

notes: Reheat gently to avoid splitting if yogurt was added.
For best freezer results, freeze before adding yogurt.
Thaw overnight in fridge; reheat gently with a splash of water.

leftoversPlan: Day 2: serve as curry bowls over rice with cucumber/onion salad.
Day 3: turn into a quick curry wrap with flatbread and yogurt sauce.
batchNotes: Double the masala base and freeze half for a faster future curry.

notes: Cook chicken to a safe internal temperature (165°F).
Cool leftovers within 2 hours; reheat until steaming hot.
If using yogurt, add off-heat and avoid boiling after adding.
Replace yogurt with 1/2 cup coconut milk at the end.
Add curry leaves if available for a South Indian aroma.
Use pre-made onion-tomato masala (frozen) and reduce onion cook time to 5 minutes.
Use chicken breast and simmer 10–12 minutes.
